title: Support named sequence declarations and instances in assertions
body:
## Summary
This patch adds support for named sequence declarations (IEEE 1800-2023 16.7) and sequence instances (16.8) used inside assertion properties. Previously, Verilator rejected any `sequence ... endsequence` declaration with an `UNSUPPORTED` error at parse time, even for simple boolean-body sequences referenced in `assert property`. This patch moves the unsupported check from the parser to `V3AssertPre`, where sequence bodies are inlined at call sites, supporting formal/actual arguments and multiple references.

## Reproducer
```systemverilog
module t (input clk);
  logic a, b, c;

  sequence seq_ab;
    a && b;
  endsequence

  assert property (@(posedge clk) seq_ab |-> c);
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t_sequence_ref.v:14:12: Unsupported: sequence
   14 |   sequence seq_ab;
      |            ^~~~~~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.047
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

## Changes
- `src/verilog.y`: Remove early `UNSUPPORTED` error from `sequence_declaration` grammar rule; sequences are now accepted at parse time
- `src/V3Width.cpp`: Replace deletion of sequences with proper width-checking of sequence body expressions and formal arguments (`visit(AstSequence*)`)
- `src/V3AssertPre.cpp`: Add `getSequenceBodyExprp()` to extract the body expression from a sequence declaration
- `src/V3AssertPre.cpp`: Add `substituteSequenceCall()` to inline the sequence body at call sites, substituting formal arguments with actuals via `V3Task::taskConnects`
- `src/V3AssertPre.cpp`: Add `visit(AstFuncRef*)` to detect sequence references and trigger inlining
- `src/V3AssertPre.cpp`: Add post-inlining cleanup in `assertPreAll()` â€” delete inlined sequences, emit `UNSUPPORTED` for sequences still referenced in unsupported contexts
- `src/V3AstNodeOther.h`: Update `AstSequence::m_referenced` comment to document lifecycle (set by `V3LinkResolve`, cleared by `V3AssertPre`)

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_sequence_ref.{v,py}` â€” named sequence without arguments, overlapping/non-overlapping implication
- Added `test_regress/t/t_sequence_ref_args.{v,py}` â€” named sequence with formal arguments, multiple references with different actuals
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` â€” golden file updated to reflect error now emitted from `V3AssertPre` instead of parser

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
